question: Can FM-37 (LLM self-tagging inflation) be mitigated with automated structural evidence detection?
methodology: Built level_inflation_check.py: regex-based heuristic scanning L3+ lessons for structural evidence markers (verb patterns, file path references). Calibrated L3 vs L4+ thresholds based on L-1119 criteria. Wired into check.sh as NOTICE-level pre-commit guard.
expect: FM-37 moves UNMITIGATED to MINIMAL with 1+ automated defense layer. Detection tool identifies >=3 historical instances of level inflation.
actual: 48.3% of L3 lessons (42/87) flagged as suspect, consistent with L-1119 manual 45%. 0 L4+ false positives after calibration. Tool wired into check.sh (1 automated advisory layer). FM-37 UNMITIGATED to MINIMAL.
diff: Expected >=3 historical instances; found 42 (14x more than minimum). Detection rate matches L-1119 manual baseline (48.3% vs 45%). L4+ false positive rate 0%.
